more.title: So should Zimbabweans be proud that our fuel is second, and not the most expensive in Africa, Prof. Mthuli?
more.description: Source: So should Zimbabweans be proud that our fuel is second, and not the most expensive in Africa, Prof. Mthuli? At times, I wonder if we are still sane in Zimbabwe. Tendai Ruben Mbofana ​In the theater of the absurd that has become Zimbabwe’s fiscal policy, Finance Minister Professor Mthuli Ncube has just delivered his [...] The post So should Zimbabweans be proud that our fuel is second, and not the most expensive in Africa, Prof. Mthuli? appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: What makes a corrupt despotic leader think he will succeed where other despots have failed?
more.description: Source: What makes a corrupt despotic leader think he will succeed where other despots have failed? Some human behaviors seemingly defy logic. Tendai Ruben Mbofana The enduring tragedy of modern governance is the recurring delusion that an iron fist can provide a permanent grip on power. If you value my social justice advocacy and writing, [...] The post What makes a corrupt despotic leader think he will succeed where other despots have failed? appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Beyond the Five-Year Horizon: Rethinking Zimbabwe’s electoral cycle
more.description: Zimbabwe’s current debate around constitutional reform, particularly the proposal to move toward a seven-year parliamentary presidency under Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 (CA3), is not emerging in a vacuum. It is rooted in a long and often turbulent electoral history, where cycles of contestation, instability, and economic disruption have shaped the country’s political trajectory. A [...] The post Beyond the Five-Year Horizon: Rethinking Zimbabwe’s electoral cycle appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Poison In Delta Beverages’ Bottle? The Explosive Court Battle Threatening To Destroy the Drinks & Beer Giant
more.description: Harare – Delta Beverages, a titan in Zimbabwe’s drinks and beer industry, finds itself embroiled in a legal maelstrom that threatens to severely tarnish its long-standing reputation. The company, a household name across the nation, has been dragged before the courts following grave allegations of distributing contaminated Coca-Cola bottles, which reportedly led to serious health [...] The post Poison In Delta Beverages’ Bottle? The Explosive Court Battle Threatening To Destroy the Drinks & Beer Giant first appeared on My Zimbabwe News .

more.title: Zimbabwe to Return 67 European-Owned Farms as Harare Pushes for Debt Relief
more.description: HARARE – The Zimbabwean government says it is in the process of returning 67 farms previously seized under the country’s controversial land reform programme to European nationals protected under bilateral investment agreements, in a significant policy shift aimed at repairing relations with Western governments and unlocking long-delayed international debt relief.
